text: We report a case of reversal omegaloop gastric bypass into normal anatomy by robotic approach in a patient who presented malnutrition after omegaloop gastric bypass, also called mini gastric bypass. A sixty nine year old female who had undergone laparoscopic omegaloop gastric bypass in March, uh, twenty forty. She had obstructive sleep apnea and type two diabetes.

In September, she had lost about a kilo with persistent diarrhea and a laboratory test showed hypoalbuminemia and anemia. The weight loss curve is shown in this graph. After a period of parenteral nutrition, she had improved her nutritional status.

The surgical team prepares for a reverse omega loop gastric bypass using a robotic approach. Six trocars are inserted: a 12 mm port for the camera, two 8 mm ports for the robotic arms, two 12 mm ports for stapling and assistance, and a 5 mm port for liver retraction. The robot is docked at the patient's left shoulder.
The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the left lobe of the liver, exposing the gastro-jejunostomy site. The gastrogenostomy is then transected using robotic instruments with purple staples.

The surgeon restores gastric continuity by performing a linear staple side-to-side anastomosis between the proximal gastric pouch and the excluded stomach. An anisogastric tube is inserted through the gastric pouch into the excluded stomach to ensure patency of the anastomosis. The stapler entry hole is closed using an unabsorbable barbed suture.

The surgeon resects the omega-loop jejunum and performs a side-to-side anastomosis using staples to prevent intestinal stenosis. Following this, the mesenteric defect is closed with a running, non-absorbable suture.
The surgeon assesses anastomotic leakage by injecting methylene blue-stained saline through an orogastric tube, followed by reinforcement of the gastrogastric anastomosis with biological glue.

The contrast upper gastrointestinal study on the first postoperative day showed that the contrast drained well into the excluded stomach. Post-operative course was uneventful and the patient was discharged on day eight. One month after the procedure, she has gained ten kilograms and stabilized nutritional status without further nutritional support.
